THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT 0522 PM CDT APPROXIMATELY 10 MILES NORTH OF PANTEX. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 3.55 MILES AND THE PATH WIDTH WAS 50.0 YARDS. THE TORNADO LIFTED AT 0533 PM CDT 11 MILES NORTH NORTHEAST OF PANTEX. SINCE THIS TORNADO REMAINED IN THE OPEN COUNTRY GRASSLANDS AND DID NO VISIBLE DAMAGE, IT WAS GIVEN A RATING OF EF-UNKNOWN AS WELL AS AN UNKNOWN ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEED.
TORNADO LASTED ABOUT 2 MINUTES.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT 0550 PM CDT APPROXIMATELY 10 MILES NORTH OF SUNRAY. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 0.21 MILE AND THE PATH WIDTH WAS 20.0 YARDS. THE TORNADO MOVED VERY LITTLE, AND LIFTED AT 0558 PM CDT 10 MILES NORTH OF SUNRAY. THIS TORNADO WAS RATED EF-0 WITH AN ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEED OF 75 MPH.

*** 2 FATAL *** NEAR ZERO VISIBILITY DUE TO BLOWING DUST CAUSED AN ACCIDENT INVOLVING 3 SEMI-TRUCKS, WITH TRAILERS AND TRUCK CAMPERS BECOMING INVOLVED IN A FIRE. WIND SPEED ACCORDING TO NEAREST MESONET WAS GUSTING UP TO 60 MPH AT TIME OF CRASH ON U.S. HIGHWAY 54.

A BRIEF LANDSPOUT TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AROUND 759 PM AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR LESS THAN 1 MINUTE. THE TORNADO SNAPPED LARGE TREE LIMBS AND DAMAGED FENCES AND SHEDS AS IT TRAVELED LESS THAN 1/4 MILE ON A SOUTHEAST HEADING. NWS SURVEY CREW RATED THE TORNADO AN EF0.

THE EFU TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN OPEN COUNTRY AROUND 7 MILES WEST-NORTHWEST OF GRUVER AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR ABOUT 4 MINUTES AND AROUND 1.3 MILES AS IT MOVED EAST-SOUTHEAST IN OPEN COUNTRY ENCOUNERING NO DAMAGE INDICATORS. THE TORNADO WAS DOCUMENTED WITH PHOTOS BY TWO SEPERATE INDIVIDUALS INCLUDING A STORM CHASER IN THE AREA.

EF1 TORNADO WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 1.84 MILES AND PATH WIDTH OF 200 YARDS TOUCHED DOWN 8 MILES WEST NORTHWEST OF MOBEETIE IN GRAY COUNTY. THE TORNADO DID MINOR DAMAGE AROUND A RESIDENCE WHICH INCLUDED VERY MINOR DAMAGE TO THE HOME ROOF CAP, A ROLLED AND DISPLACED AIR CONDITIONER UNIT, AND DESTROYED FREE STANDING ANTENNA TOWER. FOUR 10 INCH POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED WHERE THE TORNADO CROSSED A NEARBY COUNTY ROAD. A DAMAGE SURVEY WAS DONE ON AUGUST 17.
